raw:
```json
{
  "criterion": "api_design",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "OpenAPI 3.1 is valid (per swagger-cli log) and every implemented route is documented. URLs follow REST conventions consistently — plural nouns, hierarchical paths (`/api/loans/:id/return`, `/api/fines/:id/pay`), no verbs like `borrowBook`. Status codes line up with the REQUIREMENTS pattern: 402 for fine limit, 403 for suspended, 409 for loan-limit / duplicate / unavailable, all flowing through a single `DomainError` hierarchy (errors.ts:1-39) and a single middleware in routes.ts:163-170 emitting one consistent shape `{error: string}` documented as `components/schemas/Error`. The probe confirms behavior: 404 returns `{\"error\":\"Book not found\"}`, 400 returns `{\"error\":\"Invalid request\"}`. Bodies are zod-validated (routes.ts:21-41).\n\nWhy not 4: validation rejection returns only the generic message `\"Invalid request\"` (routes.ts:49-55) — no field-level detail, which the level-3 anchor explicitly calls for. Several OpenAPI gaps: POST `/api/books`, `/api/members`, `/api/loans`, `/api/reservations` can all return 400 (per probe lines 4-5, 9-10) but the spec omits 400 from those operations; POST `/api/loans` can also throw `NotFoundError` (404) for missing book/member but the spec lists only 402/403/409. Response schemas for `/api/loans/{id}/return`, `/api/reservations/expire`, and the 200 from `/api/fines/{id}/pay`'s peers are thin or missing — `return` is just `description: \"Returned loan and optional fine\"` with no schema (openapi.yaml:124-128), `expire` is `description: \"Expiry summary\"` with no schema (openapi.yaml:148-150). The reservations POST request body reuses `LoanCreate` instead of a `ReservationCreate` schema (openapi.yaml:135-136). No stable error `code` field, no negative-AC examples, no idempotency markers — all level-4 hallmarks absent. Positives that nearly reach 4/5: lists return plain arrays (no fake `total`), mutations return the resulting resource, `expireStaleReservations` is exposed both as POST and triggered idempotently from GETs (routes.ts:89, 112), and the error mapping lives cleanly in one place. Solidly reasonable but not polished enough for 4."
}
```

parsed.rationale:
OpenAPI 3.1 is valid (per swagger-cli log) and every implemented route is documented. URLs follow REST conventions consistently — plural nouns, hierarchical paths (`/api/loans/:id/return`, `/api/fines/:id/pay`), no verbs like `borrowBook`. Status codes line up with the REQUIREMENTS pattern: 402 for fine limit, 403 for suspended, 409 for loan-limit / duplicate / unavailable, all flowing through a single `DomainError` hierarchy (errors.ts:1-39) and a single middleware in routes.ts:163-170 emitting one consistent shape `{error: string}` documented as `components/schemas/Error`. The probe confirms behavior: 404 returns `{"error":"Book not found"}`, 400 returns `{"error":"Invalid request"}`. Bodies are zod-validated (routes.ts:21-41).

Why not 4: validation rejection returns only the generic message `"Invalid request"` (routes.ts:49-55) — no field-level detail, which the level-3 anchor explicitly calls for. Several OpenAPI gaps: POST `/api/books`, `/api/members`, `/api/loans`, `/api/reservations` can all return 400 (per probe lines 4-5, 9-10) but the spec omits 400 from those operations; POST `/api/loans` can also throw `NotFoundError` (404) for missing book/member but the spec lists only 402/403/409. Response schemas for `/api/loans/{id}/return`, `/api/reservations/expire`, and the 200 from `/api/fines/{id}/pay`'s peers are thin or missing — `return` is just `description: "Returned loan and optional fine"` with no schema (openapi.yaml:124-128), `expire` is `description: "Expiry summary"` with no schema (openapi.yaml:148-150). The reservations POST request body reuses `LoanCreate` instead of a `ReservationCreate` schema (openapi.yaml:135-136). No stable error `code` field, no negative-AC examples, no idempotency markers — all level-4 hallmarks absent. Positives that nearly reach 4/5: lists return plain arrays (no fake `total`), mutations return the resulting resource, `expireStaleReservations` is exposed both as POST and triggered idempotently from GETs (routes.ts:89, 112), and the error mapping lives cleanly in one place. Solidly reasonable but not polished enough for 4.
